info.title: Checkrd Control Plane API
info.description: REST API for the Checkrd control plane. Manages agents, policies, API keys, alerts, and workspace metadata. Generated from the Rust handler signatures via utoipa — do not edit schemas/api/openapi.json by hand.

get.description: Public — completes the WorkOS OAuth round-trip. Exchanges `code` for user identity, upserts the user, auto-creates a workspace if needed, issues an EdDSA JWT, and redirects to the dashboard with httpOnly session cookies set.

302.description: Redirect to the dashboard. Sets `checkrd_session` (access JWT) and `checkrd_refresh` (refresh token) httpOnly cookies.

post.description: Authenticated via httpOnly session cookie (`checkrd_session`). Called by the dashboard's `/login/device` page after the user signs in via WorkOS.

200.description: Approval recorded. The CLI's next `/auth/cli/device/token` poll will return `status: approved` with the freshly minted API key.

404.description: Code unknown, already approved/denied, or expired (intentionally generic to avoid leaking which `user_code`s existed)

post.description: Public — first leg of the RFC 8628 device-authorization grant used by `checkrd login`. No authentication required (the CLI bootstraps the flow).

200.description: Device code allocated. Show `user_code` to the user, open `verification_uri_complete` in their browser, and start polling `/auth/cli/device/token` with `device_code`.

post.description: Authenticated via httpOnly session cookie (`checkrd_session`). User pressed Deny on the dashboard's `/login/device` page.

200.description: Denial recorded. The CLI's next `/auth/cli/device/token` poll will return `status: denied`.

404.description: Code unknown, already approved/denied, or expired (intentionally generic)

post.description: Public — the device_code itself is the bearer secret. CLI polls this every `interval` seconds until a terminal status (`approved`, `denied`, `expired`).

200.description: Tagged response (`status` discriminator): one of `pending`, `slow_down`, `denied`, `expired`, or `approved`. Every response is 200 — the CLI switches on `status`.

get.description: Public — initiates the WorkOS OAuth login round-trip. No authentication required.

302.description: Redirect to the WorkOS AuthKit authorize endpoint with a CSRF state token. The browser is expected to follow the redirect and ultimately land back at `/auth/callback`.

500.description: Failed to issue CSRF state token

get.description: Authenticated via httpOnly session cookies (`checkrd_session` and/or `checkrd_refresh`). Best-effort: anonymous calls (no cookies) still clear cookies and redirect.

302.description: Clears `checkrd_session` and `checkrd_refresh` cookies, then redirects to either WorkOS RP-Initiated Logout (when a `workos_session_id` is recoverable) or the dashboard `/login` page.

200.description: The authenticated user's profile + active-org context. Used by the dashboard ProfileMenu since the JWT cookie is httpOnly and opaque to JS.

post.description: Authenticated via httpOnly refresh cookie (`checkrd_refresh`). Atomic revoke-and-return guarantees only one of two concurrent refresh requests with the same token can succeed.

200.description: New access JWT and rotated refresh token. Sets fresh `checkrd_session` and `checkrd_refresh` cookies.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or already-revoked refresh token

post.description: Authenticated via httpOnly session cookie (`checkrd_session`). Reissues the access JWT scoped to the requested org.

200.description: New JWT scoped to the target org. Also sets a refreshed `checkrd_session` cookie.

403.description: Caller is not a member of the target org

get.description:
Always returns HTTP 200 (ALB liveness probe). The response body includes
component status so operators can detect degraded Redis without the ALB
pulling the instance out of service (Redis is non-critical — the API
degrades to local-only rate limiting and no real-time SSE updates).

200.description: Process is alive. Component fields surface degraded dependencies.

post.description: Receives invitation lifecycle events from WorkOS — verified via Svix HMAC signature header (`workos-signature`). The OpenAPI security entry is empty because authentication is body-bound, not bearer-token. The handler reads only `id` (idempotency) and `event` (dispatch); all security-relevant fields (organization_id, role, email) come from our local `invitations` table, never the payload.

200.description: Event accepted (also returned for malformed handler errors so WorkOS does not infinitely retry a broken event)

503.description: Orphan invitation — local row missing; retry expected after reconciliation sweeper backfills

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return (default 20, max 100).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Cursor for the next page (UUID of the last item from the previous page).

200.description: Paginated list of agents in the caller's workspace
Agent created
Agent record
Updated agent record
Agent soft-deleted

Long-lived SSE stream emitting `init` (current state on connect), `kill_switch` (toggled), and `policy_updated` (DSSE-signed bundle) events. Polling fallback at `.../control/state`. Each `policy_updated` event carries a DSSE envelope verified in-WASM by the SDK.

get.summary: JWT-authenticated SSE stream for the dashboard.
get.description:
Same payload shape as the SDK-facing `/control` stream (init event +
`kill_switch` / `policy_updated` events) but uses the dashboard's JWT
cookie auth instead of the SDK `X-API-Key` header. EventSource in
browsers sends same-origin cookies automatically, so there's no
client-side token plumbing — the cookie just needs to be present.

Lives at a distinct path (`/control/dashboard-stream`) so dashboard
middleware (CSRF / rate-limit tier) applies naturally via the
`/v1/*` prefix, and so SDKs can't accidentally consume a JWT-gated
endpoint.

200.description: JWT/cookie-authenticated SSE stream for the dashboard. Same `init` / `kill_switch` / `policy_updated` event payloads as the SDK stream — distinct path so SDKs cannot accidentally consume a JWT-gated endpoint.
Current kill-switch state plus the DSSE-signed active policy envelope. Polling fallback for SDKs that cannot hold a long-lived SSE connection.
Kill switch toggled

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return (default 20, max 100).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Cursor for the next page (UUID of the last item from the previous page).

200.description: Paginated policy versions, newest first

404.description: Agent not found in caller's org

200.description: Policy version created (does not auto-activate)

404.description: Agent not found in caller's org

200.description: Currently active policy for this agent
Static-analysis findings (with line numbers + optional auto-fix suggestions)

404.description: Agent not found in caller's org

200.description: Terraform-plan-style diff between the base version and the candidate YAML. Mirrors `checkrd_shared::PolicyDiff`.

404.description: Base version does not exist for this agent

get.summary:
Returns the merged effective policy and per-line source map.
The source_map marks each line as originating from "org" or "agent".
If no org policy exists, returns the agent's active policy directly.
If no active policy at all, returns 404.

200.description: Merged effective policy YAML with per-line source attribution

404.description: No org policy and no active agent policy

200.description: Replay summary with verdict counts and the set of events whose verdict would change

400.description: Policy YAML failed validation, or `range` is unsupported

404.description: Agent not found in caller's org

200.description: Test summary with per-case pass/fail. Mirrors `checkrd_shared::PolicyTestSummary`.

400.description: Policy YAML failed validation, or test cases exceeded limits

404.description: Agent not found in caller's org

200.description: Specific policy version

404.description: Policy version does not exist for this agent

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Policy version (must be a draft, not active)

200.description: Updated draft policy

404.description: Policy version does not exist for this agent

409.description: Policy version is active and cannot be edited (create a new version instead)

200.description: Activated policy. Exactly one policy per agent is active at a time — the prior active version is deactivated atomically.

404.description: Policy version does not exist for this agent

200.description: Key registered or idempotent replay

409.description: Agent already has a different key registered

parameters.name: period
parameters.description:
Sentry-style window shorthand: `1h`, `24h`, `7d`, `30d`.
Applied if `from` is absent. Ignored otherwise.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor from a prior response's `next_cursor`.

200.description: Paginated alert state transitions for the caller's workspace, newest first

400.description: Invalid filter (period, time range, cursor, or list bounds)

parameters.name: bucket
parameters.description:
Bucket size: `1h`, `6h`, `1d`. Auto-selected from the window
if omitted.

200.description: Time-bucketed counts of alert state transitions, sorted ascending

400.description: Invalid filter (period, time range, bucket, or list bounds)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return (default 20, max 100).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description:
Cursor for the next page (UUID of the last item from the
previous page).

parameters.name: agent_id
parameters.description: Restrict to alert rules belonging to a specific agent.

parameters.name: alert_state
parameters.description:
Comma-separated state values, e.g. `ok,resolved,no_data`.
Accepts a single value too, for backwards compatibility with
earlier clients that only sent one state at a time.

200.description: Paginated list of alert rules in the caller's workspace
Alert rule created

404.description: Agent not found in caller's workspace

200.description: Updated alert rule
Alert rule deleted

Alert rule muted

Alert rule enabled / disabled
Alert rule unmuted

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return (default 20, max 100).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description:
Cursor for the next page (UUID of the last item from the
previous page).

parameters.name: actor_id
parameters.description: Filter by the UUID of the acting user.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description:
Case-insensitive substring match across user name, user
email, and stringified `resource_id`.

200.description: Paginated list of audit log entries for the caller's workspace

402.description: Plan does not include audit log access

200.description: Up to 100 most-recent audit entries for the resource (newest first)

402.description: Plan does not include audit log access

200.description: Stripe-hosted Checkout Session URL the client redirects to

500.description: Stripe is not configured or the upstream call failed

200.description: Stripe Customer Portal URL the client redirects to

500.description: Stripe is not configured or the upstream call failed

200.description: Plan tier, subscription state, live resource counts, and monthly event usage

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Window start, RFC3339 timestamp. Must be paired with `to`.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Window end, RFC3339 timestamp. Must be paired with `from`.

200.description: Per-agent rollup stats ordered by name

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return (default 20, max 100).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor for the next page (base64-encoded EventCursor).

parameters.name: agent_id
parameters.description: Filter to events from a single agent.

parameters.name: method
parameters.description:
HTTP method filter. Comma-separated for multi-value
(`method=GET,POST`). Tokens are case-insensitive on input.

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: Substring filter on URL host (case-insensitive, max 253 chars).

parameters.name: status_class
parameters.description:
HTTP status bucket filter: `2xx`, `4xx`, or `5xx`. The dashboard
sends this as `status=<bucket>`; the longer `status_class` alias
is accepted for explicit API consumers.

parameters.name: policy_result
parameters.description:
Policy result filter: `allowed`, `denied`, or `error`. The
shorter `result` alias is accepted for explicit API consumers.

parameters.name: trace_id
parameters.description: W3C trace_id (32 lowercase hex chars) for trace-scoped lookup.

200.description: Paginated telemetry event feed for the workspace

get.summary:
Look up a single telemetry event by its request_id, scoped to the
authenticated user's org. Returns 404 if no matching event exists.
get.description:
The request_id is the UUID emitted by the SDK per intercepted request
and stored in ClickHouse. It is org-scoped — an event from another org
with the same request_id is never returned.

200.description: Single telemetry event

404.description: No event with this request_id in the caller's org

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of hosts to return. Default 10, max 50.

200.description: Top external API hosts with allow/deny/error breakdown

get.summary: Per-rule hit counts with a sparkline-ready hourly histogram.
get.description:
Stripe Radar pattern: "rule X matched Y times in the last N hours" with a
24-point spark so dead rules are obvious at a glance. `agent_id` scopes
to a single agent's evaluations; when omitted the response aggregates
across every agent in the org.

parameters.name: agent_id
parameters.description:
Scope to a single agent. When omitted, aggregates across the
org.

200.description: Per-rule hit counts with hourly sparkline data

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Window start, RFC3339 timestamp. Must be paired with `to`.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Window end, RFC3339 timestamp. Must be paired with `from`.

200.description: Org-wide aggregate counters and latency percentiles for the window

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Window start, RFC3339 timestamp. Must be paired with `to`.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Window end, RFC3339 timestamp. Must be paired with `from`.

200.description: Time-bucketed counts for the dashboard chart

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return (default 20, max 100).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Cursor for the next page (UUID of the last item from the previous page).

200.description: Paginated list of API keys for the caller's workspace
API key created — `key` is shown exactly once
Key permanently deleted

409.description: Key is still active — call /revoke first

200.description: Key revoked

404.description: Key not found or already revoked

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return (default 20, max 100).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Cursor for the next page (UUID of the last item from the previous page).

200.description: Paginated list of org policy versions, newest first
Org policy version created (does not auto-activate). The response also embeds the static-analysis report for the new YAML.
Currently active org policy
Static-analysis report for the candidate YAML. Carries enriched `findings` plus the legacy `warnings`/`summary` fields kept for forward-compat.

get.summary:
Lists every agent in the caller's org that inherits the active org policy.
Every non-deleted agent inherits automatically — there is no opt-out
today — so this is effectively a per-agent summary of (name, active
agent-policy version, active agent-policy mode). Readable by any
authenticated org member (viewer+).

200.description: Per-agent summary of inheritance state

post.summary:
Replay the candidate org policy against recent events from *every*
inheriting agent in the workspace. Aggregates verdict counts and
surfaces the set of events whose verdict would change under the new
policy. Stateless — a fresh `RateLimiter` per event matches the
per-agent replay semantics (treat `would_rate_limit` as approximate).
post.description:
Any authenticated member can trigger this (viewer+). We enforce the
same org-scope gate as the other org-policies routes via `live_role`.

200.description: Replay summary with verdict counts and the set of events whose verdict would change

400.description: Policy YAML failed validation, or `range` is unsupported

post.summary:
Run inline policy tests against an org policy YAML. Stateless — the
handler validates + evaluates the candidate YAML directly via the core
test runner, identical to the agent-scoped `test_policy`. Gated to
viewer+ because tests never mutate state. See
`POST /v1/agents/:id/policies/test` for the analogous agent endpoint.

200.description: Test summary with per-case pass/fail. Mirrors `checkrd_shared::PolicyTestSummary` — see the per-agent test endpoint for the canonical shape.

400.description: Policy YAML failed validation, or test cases exceeded limits

200.description: Activated policy. Exactly one org policy is active at a time — the prior active version is deactivated atomically.

404.description: Version does not exist for this org

get.summary: List every workspace the caller is a member of.
get.description:
Returns the active org id (from the JWT) alongside the list so
the dashboard's switcher can render in a single round-trip.

200.description: All workspaces the caller belongs to

post.summary: Create a new workspace owned by the caller.
post.description:
Per-user cap of 5 free workspaces (Supabase pattern); paid
workspaces don't count against the cap. Sliding-window rate limit
of 10 creates per hour per user via Redis (degrades open).

Returns `org_count_exceeded` (400) when the free-org cap is hit
and `org_rate_limited` (429) when the per-hour limit fires.

200.description: Workspace created

400.description: Invalid name or per-user free-org cap exceeded (`org_count_exceeded`)

put.summary: Rename a workspace. Requires the Admin role.

200.description: Updated workspace

400.description: Invalid name (empty after trim or >100 chars)

403.description: Caller lacks the Admin role or is not a member

delete.summary:
Soft-delete a workspace. Requires the Owner role. Blocked when
the workspace is the caller's last owned workspace (would leave
the user orgless and unable to authenticate) — returns
`cannot_delete_last_org` (400) in that case.

200.description: Workspace soft-deleted

400.description: Workspace is the caller's last owned workspace (`cannot_delete_last_org`)

403.description: Caller lacks the Owner role or is not a member

get.summary:
List a workspace's invitations, newest first. Forward-only
cursor pagination on `(created_at DESC, id DESC)`.
get.description:
Filter by status with `?status=pending` (single) or
`?status=pending,accepted` (multiple). Unknown statuses return
400 (`invalid_query_parameter`) rather than silently empty pages.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description:
Opaque cursor returned as `list_metadata.after` from the previous
page. Clients MUST NOT interpret the value; round-trip it verbatim.
Format is base64url-encoded JSON `{"c":<rfc3339>,"i":<uuid>}`.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
Page size. Clamped server-side to 1..=100 (WorkOS's historical max
to stay wire-compatible with pre-Phase-4 clients).

parameters.name: status
parameters.description:
Comma-separated status filter. e.g. `?status=pending` or
`?status=pending,accepted`. Empty/missing returns all.

200.description: Page of invitations with cursor metadata

400.description: Invalid cursor or unknown status filter

403.description: Caller is not a member of the workspace

post.summary: Send an invitation to join a workspace. Requires the Admin role.
post.description:
Side effects: creates a WorkOS organization on first use, sends
the invitation email via WorkOS, and persists a local pending
invitation row. All three are atomic from the caller's
perspective — the WorkOS round-trip is wrapped by a Postgres
transaction with an advisory lock that serializes concurrent
sends from the same workspace.

Per-org rate limit (Free: 5/hr, Team: 20/hr, Enterprise: none) and
seat-cap enforcement (members + pending invites <= plan limit).
Returns `conflict_already_invited` (409) if a pending invitation
already exists for the email.

200.description: Invitation created and email sent

403.description: Caller lacks the Admin role or is not a member

409.description: A pending invitation already exists for this email (`conflict_already_invited`)

post.summary: Resend an invitation. Requires the Admin role.
post.description:
Implemented as revoke + new send: WorkOS' own resend endpoint
doesn't reliably refresh `expires_at`, and customers expect a
fresh expiration on resend. The original row is transitioned to
`"revoked"` and a new pending row is inserted, both audited.

200.description: New invitation issued; the prior row is now revoked

403.description: Caller lacks the Admin role or is not a member

404.description: Invitation not found in this workspace (`invitation_not_found`)

409.description: Invitation is already in a terminal state (`invitation_invalid_state`)

post.summary: Revoke a pending invitation. Requires the Admin role.
post.description:
WorkOS-first ordering: the upstream invitation is revoked before
the local row, so a partial failure leaves the email link dead
(the safe direction). Only `"pending"` invitations can be
revoked; accepted/revoked/expired return
`invitation_invalid_state` (409).

200.description: Invitation revoked

403.description: Caller lacks the Admin role or is not a member

404.description: Invitation not found in this workspace (`invitation_not_found`)

409.description: Invitation is already in a terminal state (`invitation_invalid_state`)

get.summary:
List every member of a workspace. Any member of the workspace
(regardless of role) can view the roster.

200.description: Workspace members

403.description: Caller is not a member of the workspace

delete.summary: Remove a member from a workspace. Requires the Admin role.
delete.description:
Cannot remove an owner — the DELETE statement is scoped with
`role != 'owner'`, so attempting to remove an owner returns 404
the same as a non-existent membership. To demote an owner first,
use `PUT /v1/orgs/{org_id}/members/{member_id}/role`.

parameters.name: member_id
parameters.description: Membership row ID (NOT the user ID)

200.description: Member removed

403.description: Caller lacks the Admin role or is not a member

404.description: Membership not found, or the target is an owner (owners cannot be removed directly)

put.summary: Update a member's role. Requires the Admin role.
put.description:
The mutation is linearizable: the implementation locks every
owner row of the workspace before evaluating the
"is this the last owner" check inside a single CTE statement,
so two concurrent demotions of two distinct owners cannot both
succeed. Demoting the last remaining owner returns a 422
validation error (`last_owner`).

parameters.name: member_id
parameters.description: Membership row ID (NOT the user ID)

200.description: Role updated

403.description: Caller lacks the Admin role or is not a member

404.description: Membership not found in this workspace

422.description: Update would demote the last owner of the workspace

200.description: Built-in policy template catalog
Rendered policy YAML

400.description: Unknown template, missing required parameter, or rendered YAML failed validation

requestBody.description: Raw Stripe event payload. Authenticated via the `Stripe-Signature` header (HMAC-SHA256 of `{timestamp}.{body}` against the webhook signing secret). Replay-protected via a 5-minute timestamp tolerance per Stripe's recommendation.

200.description: Event processed (or duplicate / unhandled type — Stripe should not retry)

400.description: Body not UTF-8, unparseable, or signature verification failed

500.description: Internal error recording the event for idempotency

200.description: Batch accepted (returns count of events durably stored)

400.description: Invalid JSON body, oversized batch (>1000), invalid event, or invalid signature

429.description: Per-org telemetry rate limit exceeded for the caller's plan tier

Agent.description:
An agent — one autonomous AI process under policy enforcement.

Returned from `GET /v1/agents`, `GET /v1/agents/{id}`,
`POST /v1/agents`, `PUT /v1/agents/{id}`,
`POST /v1/agents/{id}/kill-switch`.

active_policy_mode.description:
The enforcement mode of the agent's currently active policy.
`"dry_run"` means policy decisions are logged but always allowed.
`"enforce"` (or `null` when no active policy exists) means decisions
are applied normally. Derived from the active policy's YAML `mode:` key.

AgentStats.description:
`GET /v1/dashboard/agents` response item.

Per-agent rollup for the agents list view. Returned as
`Vec<AgentStats>` ordered by `agent_name` ascending.

total_calls.description: Total telemetry events emitted by this agent in the window.

denied_calls.description: Subset where the policy result was `denied`.

AlertHistoryBucket.description:
One time-bucketed aggregate over alert state transitions.

Returned (as a sorted slice) from
`GET /v1/alert-history/timeseries`.

AlertHistoryEntry.description:
A single alert state transition.

Returned (paginated) from `GET /v1/alert-history`.

agent_name.description: Denormalized so clients don't re-query per row.

observed_value.description:
Most-recent observed value at evaluation time. May be null if
the rule had no data.

threshold.description: Threshold the rule was evaluated against.

reason.description: Human-readable explanation of why the transition fired.

AlertNotificationEntry.description: Notification delivery log entry (sub-resource of an alert rule).

AlertRule.description:
An alert rule with all fields including evaluation state.

Returned from `POST /v1/alerts`, `PUT /v1/alerts/{id}`,
`POST /v1/alerts/{id}/toggle`, `POST /v1/alerts/{id}/mute`,
`POST /v1/alerts/{id}/unmute`.

channel_target.description: Channel target: email address or webhook URL.

AlertRuleWithAgent.description:
Alert rule joined with agent name for display in list views.

Returned (paginated) from `GET /v1/alerts`.

agent_name.description: Denormalized so the dashboard doesn't re-query per row.

AlertStateHistoryEntry.description: State transition history entry (sub-resource of an alert rule).

AnalyzeFinding.description: One enriched finding from static policy analysis.

message.description:
Human-readable explanation, shaped for direct display in the
dashboard.

rule_name.description:
Name of the offending rule, when the finding is rule-scoped.
`None` for config-level findings (e.g. `"(default)"` warnings).

line.description:
1-based line number of the offending rule in the submitted
YAML. `None` when the line cannot be located (e.g. config-
level warnings, or the rule was generated by templating).

oneOf.description:
Mechanically-applicable fix when one exists. `None` when the
remediation requires a judgment call (e.g. `overly_broad_allow`
— we can't know the intended restriction).

yaml_content.description: Candidate YAML to run static analysis against. Not persisted.

AnalyzeOrgPolicyResponse.description:
`POST /v1/org-policies/analyze` response body.

Carries enriched findings (with line numbers + auto-fix
suggestions where available) plus the legacy `warnings` /
`summary` fields kept for forward-compat with older callers.

findings.description:
Enriched findings — same shape as the per-agent analyze endpoint.
Each finding carries severity, code, message, optional rule
name, optional 1-based line number, and optional auto-fix.

yaml_content.description: Candidate YAML to run static analysis against. Not persisted.

ApiKeySummary.description:
API key summary (returned on list — never includes the full key).

Returned from `GET /v1/keys`. The full key value is only ever
returned once, from `POST /v1/keys`; afterwards the caller sees
only the prefix.

permissions.description:
Free-form JSON describing the key's permission grants. Default
is `{}` (full org permissions).

AuditLogEntryWithUser.description:
Audit log entry joined with user email/name for display.

Returned from `GET /v1/audit-log` and
`GET /v1/audit-log/{resource_type}/{resource_id}`.

resource_type.description:
Logical resource bucket the action targeted, e.g., `agent`,
`policy`, `api_key`.

details.description:
Free-form JSON payload with action-specific context (names,
before/after diffs, kill-switch reasons, etc.).

ip_address.description:
Source IP address of the request that triggered the action,
when available.

user_email.description:
Email of the user who performed the action. Null for
system-generated entries (e.g., SDK-driven public-key
registration with no user session).

user_name.description: Display name of the user who performed the action.

AutoFix.description: A single auto-fix suggestion attached to an `AnalyzeFinding`.

description.description: One-line description of the suggested change.

new_yaml.description:
Replacement YAML. Round-trips through serde so it is always
valid; YAML comments are dropped in the process.

BillingStatus.description:
`GET /v1/billing/status` response body.

Joins org-row billing state (plan tier, subscription status,
period dates) with live resource counts and the Redis-backed
monthly event counter. Powers the dashboard's `/settings/billing`
page and the `checkrd billing status` CLI command.

subscription_status.description:
Stripe subscription status. Common values: `"active"`,
`"canceling"` (synthesized when `cancel_at_period_end=true`),
`"past_due"`, `"canceled"`, `"trialing"`, `"incomplete"`.

has_stripe_customer.description:
`true` once the org has a Stripe Customer record. False until
the first checkout/portal call (lazy customer creation).

paid_until.description:
End of the current paid period (Stripe `current_period_end`).
Null when not subscribed.

trial_ends_at.description:
End of any active trial (Stripe `trial_end`). Null when not in
trial.

limits.description:
Compile-time resource limits for `plan_tier`. Sourced
server-side from `checkrd_shared::PlanTier::limits()`. Mirrored
here as a wire-shape struct because `checkrd_shared` lives in
the WASM-safe set (no `utoipa` dep) and api-types must keep
its dependency graph minimal — every wrapper that codegens
against the OpenAPI spec inherits this graph.

usage.description:
Live resource usage counts (current vs limit). Computed in a
single SQL query for consistency.

CheckoutResponse.description:
`POST /v1/billing/checkout` response body.

Returns a Stripe-hosted Checkout Session URL. The client redirects
the user there to enter payment details and complete subscription.

url.description:
Stripe Checkout Session URL. Single-use, expires after 24
hours. Always points to a Stripe-hosted page —
`checkout.stripe.com/...`.

ControlInit.description:
Initial event payload pushed on SSE stream open. Sent before any
pubsub event so clients never need a separate poll to learn the
current state of the agent.

kill_switch_active.description: Whether the kill switch is currently engaged. `true` ⇒ deny all.

active_policy_hash.description:
SHA-256 of the active policy YAML, lowercase hex. `None` only
when no active policy exists yet (brand-new agent before its
first policy push).

ControlKillSwitchEvent.description:
`kill_switch` SSE event payload — emitted whenever the kill
switch is toggled for the agent.

ControlPolicyUpdatedEvent.description:
`policy_updated` SSE event payload — emitted whenever a new
policy version is activated for the agent.

`policy_envelope` is a DSSE envelope (`PolicyBundle` payload type)
that the SDK verifies in-WASM against its pinned trust list before
installing the policy. Strong-from-the-ground-up: there is no
unsigned distribution path.

version.description:
Monotonic policy version. Used by the SDK to enforce rollback
protection: a bundle with `version <= last_policy_version` is
rejected.

hash.description: SHA-256 of the policy YAML, lowercase hex.

policy_envelope.description:
DSSE envelope wrapping the canonical `PolicyBundle` JSON.
Verified in-WASM by the SDK.

ControlState.description:
`GET /v1/agents/{agent_id}/control/state` response body — the
JSON polling fallback for SDKs that cannot hold an SSE
connection (e.g., serverless runtimes with hard request budgets).

kill_switch_active.description: Whether the kill switch is currently engaged.

policy_envelope.description:
DSSE-signed policy envelope. `None` only when the agent has
no active policy at all (a brand-new agent before its first
policy push). After the first policy is created, this field
is always present — strong-from-the-ground-up means there is
no unsigned distribution path.

name.description:
Display name for the agent. Must be unique within the
workspace and 1-128 characters.

description.description:
Optional free-form description. Surfaced on the dashboard's
agent detail page.
description.example: Stripe-test agent for the EU storefront

threshold.description:
Numeric threshold the condition is evaluated against. Must
be > 0.

cooldown_minutes.description:
Minimum minutes between notifications for this rule (default
60).

name.description: Display name for the API key. Visible on the dashboard.

permissions.description:
Optional permission grant JSON. When omitted the key inherits
full org permissions (`{}`).

CreateKeyResponse.description:
`POST /v1/keys` response body. The `key` field is the full
`ck_live_…` / `ck_test_…` value and is shown **exactly once**.
After this response the dashboard only ever exposes `key_prefix`.

key.description:
The full key — show once, never again. Treat as a credential
and store it securely (e.g., in a secret manager).

key_prefix.description:
Short prefix (e.g., `ck_live_abc12345`) used to identify the
key in dashboards and audit logs.

yaml_content.description:
Raw policy YAML. Validated server-side; bad input returns 400
with a `validation_error` describing the offending rule.

description.description:
Optional free-form description. Surfaces in the dashboard's
version timeline.

CreateOrgPolicyResponse.description:
`POST /v1/org-policies` response body.

The created policy version plus the static-analysis report. The
analysis is the same shape returned by `POST /v1/org-policies/analyze`.

analysis.description:
Raw `PolicyAnalysis` from the WASM core. Free-form because
the upstream type lives in `crates/shared` (no utoipa deps).

CreateOrgRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /v1/orgs`.

Trimmed and validated server-side: 1-100 characters after trim.

name.description: Display name. Trimmed; must be non-empty and ≤100 chars.

yaml_content.description:
Raw policy YAML. Validated server-side; bad input returns 400
with a `validation_error` describing the offending rule.

description.description:
Optional free-form description. Surfaces in the dashboard's
version timeline.

success.description:
Always `true`. Present for forward-compat with future
soft-delete responses that may surface a `pending` status.

DeviceApprovalRequest.description:
`POST /auth/cli/device/approve` and `POST /auth/cli/device/deny`
request body. Uses the short human-readable `user_code` because
these calls come from the dashboard, not the CLI — the user
types/pastes it from their terminal.

DeviceApprovalResponse.description:
`POST /auth/cli/device/approve` and `POST /auth/cli/device/deny`
response body. Always `success: true` on a 200; failures map to
404 (intentionally generic to avoid leaking which `user_code`s
existed).

DeviceCodeResponse.description:
`POST /auth/cli/device/code` response body.

First leg of the RFC 8628 device-authorization flow used by
`checkrd login`. The CLI shows `user_code` to the user and
opens `verification_uri_complete` in their browser; meanwhile
it polls `/auth/cli/device/token` with `device_code`.

device_code.description: Long opaque token the CLI polls with. ~160 bits of entropy.

user_code.description:
Short human-readable code (`XXXX-XXXX`) the user types or
pastes into the dashboard.

verification_uri.description:
Where the user opens the dashboard. The CLI typically opens
this directly so the `user_code` is pre-filled via the
query string.

verification_uri_complete.description: Convenience URL with the `user_code` already embedded.

interval.description: How often the CLI should poll, in seconds.

oneOf.description: User hasn't approved or denied yet — keep polling.

Polled too fast. Caller should wait at least `interval + 5`
seconds before the next poll.

TTL elapsed (or unknown / already-consumed code, bundled
here so an attacker probing device codes can't distinguish
"never existed" from "stolen-but-redeemed"). Terminal.

token.description:
The full API key — show once, store in keychain, never
echo back to the user. Matches the `POST /v1/keys` shape.

active_org_id.description:
Org the key is scoped to. Surfaced so the CLI can
include it in `whoami` without an extra round trip.

DeviceTokenResponse.description:
`POST /auth/cli/device/token` response body.

Tagged enum (`status` discriminator) covering every terminal +
non-terminal poll outcome. Mirrors the shape used by the Stripe
CLI `link` flow — every response is `200 OK`, the CLI switches
on `status`. Spec-strict OAuth2 errors (RFC 6749 §5.2) buy
nothing here because the CLI is our own first-party client.

base_version.description:
Version number of the base (before) policy. Must exist for
this agent or the call returns 404.

candidate_yaml.description:
YAML content of the candidate (after) policy. Validated
server-side before diffing.

ErrorBody.description:
The body inside the Stripe-style `{ "error": { ... } }` envelope.

Defined separately from `ApiError` (which is the runtime sum type)
because OpenAPI describes the *wire shape* clients see, not the
internal Rust enum. Serializing `ApiError` always yields exactly
this shape — see the `into_response` impl below.

code.description:
Machine-readable, fine-grained error code. See
[`ErrorCode`] for the full enumeration.

message.description: Human-readable message safe to surface to end users.

param.description:
Optional pointer to the offending request parameter (for
`invalid_request_error`).

ErrorResponse.description:
Stripe-style error envelope returned for any non-2xx response.

Wire shape: `{ "error": { "type", "code", "message", "param?" } }`.
Every error response in the API conforms to this — clients can
type-narrow against it once and reuse across every endpoint.

EventUsage.description:
Monthly event usage. Separate type from `ResourceCount` because
the limit is `u64` (Free: 100K, Team: 1M, Enterprise: `u64::MAX`)
— `ResourceCount`'s `u32` limit can't represent the unlimited
tier.

HealthStatus.description:
`GET /health` response body.

`status` is always `"ok"` while the process is alive — the ALB only
uses HTTP status, not body content, for liveness. The other fields
expose the live state of dependencies (Redis pubsub, control hub
subscription) so operators can spot a degraded-but-up node.

status.description:
Always `"ok"`. Present so older monitoring harnesses that
pattern-match on the body keep working.

redis.description:
Redis publisher state: `"connected"`, `"disconnected"`, or
`"not_configured"` (local dev / pre-Redis deployments).

subscription_active.description:
`true` when the control-plane Redis pubsub subscription is
active. When `false`, SSE clients fall back to the polling
path — kill-switch propagation may be delayed by one poll
interval.

IngestRequest.description:
`POST /v1/telemetry` request body.

Sent by the SDK in batches of up to 1,000 events per request. The
optional `sdk_version` is recorded on the row in ClickHouse so the
dashboard can surface "events received from SDK X.Y.Z" — useful
for spotting clients stuck on a buggy release.

events.description:
Up to 1,000 telemetry events per request. Each event is
validated against `checkrd_shared::telemetry::validate_telemetry_event`
before insertion — invalid events fail the whole batch with 400.
Modeled as `Vec<Object>` because the runtime type lives in the
WASM-safe `checkrd-shared` crate (no `utoipa` dep).

sdk_version.description:
Optional SDK version string (e.g., `"checkrd-py/0.4.1"`).
Surfaced on the dashboard so operators can correlate ingest
behavior with client versions.

IngestResponse.description:
`POST /v1/telemetry` response body.

Returns the count of events accepted into the store (always equal
to `events.len()` on a 200 — failures abort the whole batch).

accepted.description: Number of events durably accepted into the telemetry store.

active_policy_version.description:
Version of the agent's currently active per-agent policy. `None`
when the agent has no agent-level policy of its own (it inherits
the org policy directly).

active_policy_mode.description:
Enforcement mode of the agent's active per-agent policy
(`"dry_run"` or `"enforce"`). Extracted from the YAML `mode:`
key. `None` when the agent has no active policy.

Invitation.description:
An invitation row — local source-of-truth for the WorkOS-backed
invitation lifecycle. Returned by `POST /v1/orgs/{org_id}/invitations`,
`POST .../invitations/{id}/revoke`, `POST .../invitations/{id}/resend`,
and embedded in `InvitationListResponse`.

`status` is one of `"pending" | "accepted" | "revoked" | "expired"`;
terminal states (everything except `"pending"`) cannot be acted on.

workos_invitation_id.description:
Opaque WorkOS invitation identifier. Used internally to
reconcile webhook deliveries; clients can ignore it.

email.description: Email address as the inviter typed it (case preserved).

email_normalized.description:
`lower(trim(email))` — used for uniqueness checks. Always
matches `lower(trim(email))` on the DB side.

sent_by_user_id.description:
User who triggered the send. `null` for backfilled rows or
system-generated invites.

accepted_by_user_id.description:
Set when the invitation reaches `"accepted"`. Populated from
the WorkOS webhook payload, then resolved against
`users.workos_user_id`.

after.description:
Opaque cursor — pass back as `?after=` to fetch the next
page. Absent when the returned page is the last.

InvitationListResponse.description:
Response body for `GET /v1/orgs/{org_id}/invitations`.

Cursor-paginated; clients should round-trip
`list_metadata.after` to fetch the next page. Forward-only
(no `before` cursor — keyset reverse-paging would double the
index footprint without a clear use case).

KeyActionResponse.description:
`POST /v1/keys/{key_id}/revoke` and `DELETE /v1/keys/{key_id}`
response body. Always `success: true` on a 200.

active.description:
`true` to engage the kill switch (deny all traffic),
`false` to release.

reason.description:
Free-text reason, surfaced in the audit log so the dashboard's
RecentKillHistory can explain *why* an agent was killed.

ListOrgsResponse.description:
Response body for `GET /v1/orgs`. Lists every org the authenticated
user is a member of, plus the currently-active org from the JWT so
the dashboard's switcher can render without an extra `whoami` call.

active_org_id.description:
The org id encoded into the caller's session JWT — i.e., the
org their requests are scoped to until they switch.

MeResponse.description:
`GET /auth/me` response body.

Identity payload for the authenticated user + active org context.
The dashboard's `useAuth()` hook uses this to populate the
ProfileMenu (email + name + avatar_url) plus the active-org
context already in the JWT (active_org_id, role) so clients
don't have to cross-reference two responses.

role.description:
Caller's role in the active org. One of `owner`, `admin`,
`member`, `viewer`.

MergedEffectivePolicyResponse.description:
`GET /v1/agents/{agent_id}/policies/merged-effective` response body.

Carries the merged YAML alongside per-line source attribution so
the dashboard can render origin annotations (org / agent / both).

yaml.description: Canonical YAML of the merged effective policy.

source_map.description:
One entry per line of `yaml`, tagged with its source
(`"org"`, `"agent"`, or `"both"`).

org_yaml.description: Raw org YAML used in the merge (empty string when none exists).

agent_yaml.description: Raw agent YAML used in the merge (empty string when none exists).

has_org_policy.description: `true` when an active org policy was merged in.

org_policy_version.description:
Version of the org policy that was merged. `None` when no org
policy exists.

agent_policy_version.description:
Version of the per-agent active policy. `None` when the agent
has none.

MuteAlertRequest.description:
`POST /v1/alerts/{alert_id}/mute` request body. Provide either
`until` (RFC 3339 timestamp) or `duration_minutes` (relative).
`until` wins if both are set.

duration_minutes.description:
Minutes from now to mute. Ignored if `until` is set. Must be
> 0.

OrgMemberWithEmail.description:
A member of an organization, with denormalized user fields so
the dashboard's member list doesn't need a second roundtrip.
Returned by `GET /v1/orgs/{org_id}/members`.

accepted_at.description:
When the user accepted the invitation. `null` for legacy
rows pre-dating the invitation flow.

user_name.description:
Display name from the user record. May be `null` if the
user has not completed profile setup.

OrgPolicy.description:
A single org-policy version.

Returned from `GET /v1/org-policies` (paginated),
`GET /v1/org-policies/active`,
`POST /v1/org-policies/{version}/activate`.

version.description: Monotonic per-org version number, assigned at create time.

yaml_content.description: Raw policy YAML. Validated by the server before persisting.

hash.description:
SHA-256 of `yaml_content`, hex-encoded. Stable identifier for
equality checks and trust-list pinning.

description.description: Optional free-form description supplied at create time.

is_active.description:
Exactly one row per org has `is_active = true`. The activate
endpoint maintains the invariant in a single transaction.

created_by.description:
`users.id` of the actor who created this version. `None` for
system-created policies (rare; reserved for future automation).

yaml_content.description: Candidate YAML to replay. Not persisted.

range.description:
Time range for event lookup: `"1h"`, `"24h"`, or `"7d"`.
Defaults to `"24h"` when omitted.

limit.description: Maximum events to evaluate. Defaults to 10_000. Caps at 10_000.

total_events.description: Total events evaluated (capped by the request `limit`).

would_allow.description: Number that the candidate policy would allow.

would_deny.description: Number that the candidate policy would deny.

would_rate_limit.description:
Approximate. Replay creates a fresh `RateLimiter` per event
(stateless), so counters do not accumulate across the window.

delta_event_ids.description:
`request_id`s where the candidate's verdict differs from the
recorded `policy_result` for that event. Bounded — see the
per-agent replay endpoint for the same cap.

agents_sampled.description: Number of distinct agents whose events were included in the replay.

OrgStats.description:
`GET /v1/dashboard/stats` response body.

Org-wide aggregate counters and latency percentiles for the
requested time window. Powers the dashboard overview cards.

total_calls.description: Total telemetry events ingested in the window.

allowed_calls.description: Subset of events where the policy result was `allowed`.

denied_calls.description: Subset of events where the policy result was `denied`.

error_calls.description: Subset of events with HTTP status >= 500 (post-policy errors).

avg_latency_ms.description: Mean latency across all events in the window, in milliseconds.

active_agents.description: Distinct agents that emitted at least one event in the window.

total_agents.description:
Total agents in the workspace (active + idle, excluding soft-deleted).
Sourced from Aurora, not ClickHouse.

Organization.description:
An organization (workspace) — every Checkrd customer is one. Returned
as the wire shape for `/v1/orgs` and embedded in a number of
invitation / member responses.

`plan_tier` is the lowercase string `"free"`, `"team"`, or
`"enterprise"` (mirroring `checkrd_shared::PlanTier`'s serde repr).

slug.description:
URL-safe slug derived at create time. Stable for the
lifetime of the org.

PaginatedAgents.description:
`GET /v1/agents` response body — monomorphized
`PaginatedResponse<Agent>` for the OpenAPI document.

PaginatedAlertHistory.description:
`GET /v1/alert-history` response body — monomorphized
`PaginatedResponse<AlertHistoryEntry>` for the OpenAPI document.

PaginatedAlertNotifications.description:
`GET /v1/alerts/{alert_id}/notifications` response body —
monomorphized `PaginatedResponse<AlertNotificationEntry>` for the
OpenAPI document.

PaginatedAlertRules.description:
`GET /v1/alerts` response body — monomorphized
`PaginatedResponse<AlertRuleWithAgent>` for the OpenAPI document.

PaginatedAlertStateHistory.description:
`GET /v1/alerts/{alert_id}/history` response body — monomorphized
`PaginatedResponse<AlertStateHistoryEntry>` for the OpenAPI document.

PaginatedApiKeys.description:
`GET /v1/keys` response body — monomorphized
`PaginatedResponse<ApiKeySummary>` for the OpenAPI document.

PaginatedAuditLog.description:
`GET /v1/audit-log` response body — monomorphized
`PaginatedResponse<AuditLogEntryWithUser>` for the OpenAPI document.

PaginatedOrgPolicies.description:
`GET /v1/org-policies` response body — monomorphized
`PaginatedResponse<OrgPolicy>` for the OpenAPI document.

PaginatedPolicies.description:
`GET /v1/agents/{agent_id}/policies` response body — monomorphized
`PaginatedResponse<Policy>` for the OpenAPI document.

PaginatedTelemetryEvents.description:
`GET /v1/dashboard/events` response body — monomorphized
`PaginatedResponse<TelemetryEventRow>` for the OpenAPI document.

PlanLimits.description:
Resource counts and feature flags for a billing tier. Wire-shape
mirror of `checkrd_shared::PlanLimits` (which can't derive
`ToSchema` because it has to compile to `wasm32-wasip1`).

Both structs serialize identically — the api crate converts at
the boundary.

max_events_per_month.description:
Monthly event cap. `u64::MAX` for the unlimited tier.
Serialized as `number` for TS — JS numbers are f64 with an
upper bound of 2^53-1, still plenty for monthly events.

Policy.description:
A single per-agent policy version.

Returned from `POST /v1/agents/{agent_id}/policies` (create),
`GET /v1/agents/{agent_id}/policies` (paginated),
`GET /v1/agents/{agent_id}/policies/active`,
`GET /v1/agents/{agent_id}/policies/{version}`,
`PATCH /v1/agents/{agent_id}/policies/{version}` (draft updates),
`POST /v1/agents/{agent_id}/policies/{version}/activate`.

version.description: Monotonic per-agent version number, assigned at create time.

yaml_content.description: Raw policy YAML. Validated by the server before persisting.

hash.description:
SHA-256 of `yaml_content`, hex-encoded. Stable identifier for
equality checks and trust-list pinning.

description.description: Optional free-form description supplied at create time.

is_active.description:
Exactly one row per agent has `is_active = true`. The activate
endpoint maintains the invariant in a single transaction.

created_by.description:
`users.id` of the actor who created this version. `None` for
system-created policies (rare; reserved for future automation).

PolicyDiffResponse.description:
`POST /v1/agents/{agent_id}/policies/diff` response body.

Free-form JSON because the upstream `checkrd_shared::PolicyDiff`
lives in the shared crate, which has no utoipa dependency.
Documented inline in the OpenAPI spec; the server emits the
canonical `PolicyDiff` shape verbatim. Fields below mirror that
shape so the schema remains discoverable.

rules.description: One entry per rule with the diff verdict.

PolicyTemplate.description:
`GET /v1/policy-templates` response row.

Built-in templates only — no template body. Pair with a `render`
call (`POST /v1/policy-templates/{id}/render`) to materialize a
concrete YAML policy.

id.description:
Stable template id (e.g. `"api-allowlist"`). Used as the path
parameter on the render endpoint.

name.description: Display name. Surface on the dashboard's template picker.

description.description: One-line summary of what the template does.

category.description:
Coarse category (`"security"`, `"compliance"`, `"performance"`).
Used for grouping in the picker UI.

parameters.description: Ordered parameter list — same order the server renders them in.

param_schema.description:
JSON Schema describing the parameters accepted by this template.
The dashboard auto-generates the parameter form from this schema.

PolicyTestSummaryResponse.description:
`POST /v1/agents/{agent_id}/policies/test` response body.

Free-form JSON because the upstream `checkrd_shared::PolicyTestSummary`
lives in the shared crate, which has no utoipa dependency.
Documented inline in the OpenAPI spec; the server emits the
canonical `PolicyTestSummary` shape verbatim. Fields below mirror
that shape so the schema remains discoverable.

total.description: Total number of test cases evaluated.

results.description:
Per-test-case result rows. Each row carries `name`, `passed`,
`expected_allowed`, `actual_allowed`, and (when set)
`expected_rule`, `actual_rule`, and `error`.

PortalResponse.description:
`POST /v1/billing/portal` response body.

Returns a Stripe Customer Portal URL where the user can update
payment method, view invoices, and cancel/upgrade their
subscription.

url.description:
Stripe Customer Portal URL. Single-use, expires after the
portal session ends. Always points to a Stripe-hosted page —
`billing.stripe.com/...`.

public_key.description: Hex-encoded 32-byte Ed25519 public key (64 hex chars).

registered.description:
`true` if this call set the key for the first time, `false` if the
same key was already registered (idempotent replay).

public_key.description: The public key that is now associated with the agent (lowercase hex).

RenameOrgRequest.description:
Request body for `PUT /v1/orgs/{org_id}` (rename). Same constraints
as create.

name.description: New display name. Trimmed; must be non-empty and ≤100 chars.

RenderTemplateRequest.description:
`POST /v1/policy-templates/{id}/render` request body.

`parameters` is a free-form JSON object; keys must match the
`TemplateParam.name` entries returned by the listing endpoint.
Types are validated server-side against `TemplateParam.param_type`.

parameters.description:
Parameter values keyed by parameter name. Required parameters
without defaults must appear here or the call returns 400.

RenderTemplateResponse.description:
`POST /v1/policy-templates/{id}/render` response body.

Returns the rendered YAML alongside the template id for callers
that batch-render. The YAML is validated server-side through the
same pipeline as hand-authored policies — bad substitutions
surface as 400 with a `validation_error`.

template_id.description: Echo of the path parameter — useful when batching renders.

rendered_yaml.description:
The rendered policy YAML, ready to send to
`POST /v1/agents/{agent_id}/policies` or
`POST /v1/org-policies`.

yaml_content.description: Candidate YAML to replay against this agent's recent events.

range.description:
Time range for event lookup: `"1h"`, `"24h"`, or `"7d"`.
Defaults to `"24h"` when omitted.

limit.description: Maximum events to evaluate. Defaults to 1000. Caps at 5000.

total_events.description: Total events evaluated (capped by the request `limit`).

would_allow.description: Number that the candidate policy would allow.

would_deny.description: Number that the candidate policy would deny.

would_rate_limit.description:
Approximate. Replay creates a fresh `RateLimiter` per event
(stateless), so counters do not accumulate across the window.

delta_event_ids.description:
`request_id`s where the candidate's verdict differs from the
recorded `policy_result` for that event.

ResourceCount.description: Current count + limit pair for resource-bounded entities.

current.description:
Current count. `i64` on the wire — fine for seat counts (we
will never exceed 2^53 agents).

limit.description:
Hard cap for the current plan tier. `u32::MAX` (4 billion) for
enterprise = effectively unlimited.

ResourceUsage.description: Resource counts for a workspace, joined to its plan limits.

events_this_month.description:
Events ingested for this org in the current calendar month
(UTC). Sourced from the Redis-backed monthly quota counter,
not the DB. `current` shows `0` when Redis is unavailable;
the dashboard renders `—` rather than 500ing the page.

RuleHit.description:
`GET /v1/dashboard/rule-hits` response item.

Aggregated hit count for a single policy rule across the queried
window, plus a sparkline-ready hourly histogram. Powers the
per-rule sparklines in the policy editor (Stripe Radar pattern).

hourly_counts.description:
Histogram over the time window, oldest-to-newest. Always at
hourly granularity (downsampled to fit when the window is
wider).

email.description:
Email address of the invitee. Validated against a strict
regex; case is preserved on the wire and only normalized
internally for uniqueness checks.

role.description:
Role the invitee will receive on acceptance. One of
`"owner" | "admin" | "member" | "viewer"`.

org_id.description:
Target workspace to scope the new session to. Must be an
org the caller is a member of.

TelemetryEventRow.description:
A single telemetry event row.

Returned by `GET /v1/dashboard/events` (paginated list) and
`GET /v1/dashboard/events/{request_id}` (single lookup). Mirrors
the runtime row type in `checkrd_telemetry_store::TelemetryEventRow`.

request_id.description:
SDK-generated UUID for the intercepted request. Org-scoped —
the same `request_id` from another org is never returned.

url_path.description:
Parameterized path template (e.g., `/v1/charges/{id}`). PII is
stripped client-side before the event leaves the SDK.

deny_reason.description:
Rule name that produced a deny decision, if any. Never carries
body values — only user-authored rule identifiers.

source.description:
`"sdk_signed"` (Ed25519-signed via RFC 9421 + DSSE) or
`"otlp"` (bridged from OTLP/HTTP).

matched_rule.description: Rule name that matched during evaluation, if any.

evaluation_path.description:
JSON-serialized `Vec<EvaluationStep>`. Empty string = no path
recorded.

TemplateParam.description:
Definition of a single template parameter.

Mirrors the server-side `crate::templates::TemplateParam` shape.
The server's struct uses `&'static str` for in-binary catalogs; on
the wire the values are owned `String`s.

name.description:
Parameter name. Used as the substitution key (`{{name}}`) inside
the template's YAML body.

description.description: Human-readable description shown in the dashboard's parameter form.

required.description: Whether the parameter must be provided at render time.

default_value.description:
JSON-encoded default value. Honored when the caller omits the
parameter from the render request. `None` means there is no
default — required parameters must be supplied.

TestOrgPolicyRequest.description:
`POST /v1/org-policies/test` request body.

Either supply explicit `tests` in the request body, or omit and
let the server extract a top-level `tests:` block from `yaml_content`.

yaml_content.description: Candidate YAML to test. Not persisted.

tests.description:
Optional explicit test cases. When omitted, the server extracts
the `tests:` block embedded in `yaml_content`.

Free-form JSON because `PolicyTestCase.input.headers` is a
`Vec<(String, String)>` which neither typeshare nor utoipa
expresses cleanly. Schema is enforced server-side.

TestPolicyRequest.description:
`POST /v1/agents/{agent_id}/policies/test` request body.

Either supply explicit `tests` in the request body, or omit and
let the server extract a top-level `tests:` block from
`yaml_content`.

yaml_content.description: Candidate YAML to test. Not persisted.

tests.description:
Optional explicit test cases. When omitted, the server extracts
the `tests:` block embedded in `yaml_content`.

Free-form JSON because `PolicyTestCase.input.headers` is a
`Vec<(String, String)>` which neither typeshare nor utoipa
expresses cleanly. Schema is enforced server-side.

TimeseriesBucket.description:
`GET /v1/dashboard/timeseries` response item.

One bucket of the dashboard chart. Bucket granularity is chosen
server-side to fit the requested window (1m / 5m / 1h /...).

is_enabled.description: `true` to enable the rule, `false` to disable.

TokenResponse.description:
`POST /auth/switch-org` and `POST /auth/refresh` response body.

The new access JWT. The dashboard primarily relies on the
`Set-Cookie` headers attached to the response, but the body is
kept for parity with non-browser clients that prefer to read the
token explicitly.

token.description:
Short-lived EdDSA-signed access token. Audience
`checkrd-api`. Refresh via `/auth/refresh`.

TopHost.description:
`GET /v1/dashboard/hosts` response item.

Top external API hosts the workspace's agents are calling, with a
breakdown of allow/deny/error counts per host.

UpdateAgentRequest.description:
`PUT /v1/agents/{agent_id}` request body. Both fields are
optional; omitted fields leave the existing value in place.

name.description: New display name. Omit to leave unchanged.

description.description:
New description. Omit to leave unchanged. Pass an empty
string to clear.

UpdateAlertRequest.description:
`PUT /v1/alerts/{alert_id}` request body. All evaluation
parameters are required (full replace, not patch).

UpdateDraftPolicyRequest.description:
`PATCH /v1/agents/{agent_id}/policies/{version}` request body.

Only non-active (draft) versions can be edited. Activating a
version freezes its YAML — subsequent edits require creating a
new version.

yaml_content.description: Replacement YAML for the draft version. Validated server-side.

role.description:
New role: `"owner" | "admin" | "member" | "viewer"`.
Demoting the last remaining owner is rejected (422).

WorkosWebhookEvent.description:
`POST /webhooks/workos` request body.

Minimal envelope of an inbound WorkOS event. The handler reads
only the `id` (for idempotency dedup), the `event` discriminator
(for dispatch), and the opaque `data` blob (decoded per
event-type). Everything else (organization_id, role, email)
comes from our own `invitations` table — the payload is never
trusted as a source of truth for security-relevant fields. See
`routes/webhooks.rs` for the structural rationale.

id.description:
Stable, unique WorkOS event identifier. Used as the
idempotency key so duplicate deliveries are no-ops.

data.description:
Event-type-specific payload. Decoded per discriminator;
callers should not depend on the shape across event types.

api_key.name: X-API-Key
api_key.description: Long-lived API key (`ck_live_…` or `ck_test_…`). Generated from the dashboard or via `checkrd keys create` in the CLI.

bearer_jwt.description: Short-lived EdDSA JWT issued by /auth/callback. Audience `checkrd-api`. Refresh via /auth/refresh.

tags.name: alerts
tags.description: Alert rule CRUD, mute/toggle, history, notifications

tags.name: auth
tags.description: OAuth login, session refresh, org switch, device-code flow

tags.name: billing
tags.description: Stripe checkout, customer portal, plan / usage status

tags.name: control
tags.description: SSE control stream and polling fallback for SDK kill-switch + policy reload

tags.name: dashboard
tags.description: Read-only stats, timeseries, top-hosts, and event listings

tags.name: orgs
tags.description: Workspace CRUD, membership, role updates, invitations

tags.name: org-policies
tags.description: Org-default policies (deny rules unremovable, IAM SCP model)

tags.name: policies
tags.description: Per-agent policy versioning, diff, test, replay, analyze, merge

tags.name: templates
tags.description: Built-in policy templates and parameterized rendering

tags.name: webhooks
tags.description: Inbound webhooks from Stripe and WorkOS — HMAC-authenticated, no SDK exposure
